exit_criteria: Control artifacts exist in Athanor and are lifecycle-tracked.
Validator knows the reconciliation surface.
checkpoints.scope: Reconciliation control docs, source registry, tenant queue, ecosystem registry, validator wiring, and preservation baseline.

exit_criteria: Repo-side deployment truth is corrected where runtime and registry evidence agree.
Remaining drift is explicitly runtime-owned or packeted.
checkpoints.scope: High-confidence repo-side closure of deployment drift while pushing unresolved live-state mismatches into governed runtime debt.

exit_criteria: Harvest decisions are reflected in the reconciliation ledger and extraction queue.
No harvested source remains ambiguous about authority.
checkpoints.scope: Selective import, archive, and rejection decisions across athanor-next, Reconcile, Local-System, Agentic Coding Tools, and adjacent operator tooling roots.

exit_criteria: GitHub portfolio classification is explicit and current.
Tenant queue and ecosystem registry reflect the same decisions.
checkpoints.scope: Category-batch GitHub and local product classification into core, shared-module, tenant, lineage, operator-tooling, reference, or archive.

exit_criteria: Runtime-owned drift has an approved packet or a deliberate demotion.
Host-level repair work no longer depends on implicit operator memory.
checkpoints.scope: Approval-gated runtime packets for stale live Foundry, VAULT, DEV, and WORKSHOP configs that still contradict implementation authority.

exit_criteria: Implementation authority is singular and published.
Side roots and lineage lanes are frozen or classified.
Acceptance matrix passes at the checkpoint boundary.
checkpoints.scope: Checkpointed GitHub publication, side-root freeze, final truth normalization, and completion evidence refresh.

final_acceptance: Authority Gate: no shadow implementation authority remains.
Current-State Truth Gate: top-entry docs and Ralph state agree.
Runtime Gate: major live drift is reconciled, packet-ready, or explicitly externally blocked.
Provider Gate: all provider lanes have explicit evidence-backed posture.
Portfolio Gate: all in-scope roots and repos have final disposition.
Product Gate: Field Inspect and RFI reconciliation lanes are closed as active cleanup items.
Validation Gate: the standard validator and generated-doc suite remains green.
Steady-State Gate: reconciliation workstreams can move to steady-state monitoring or completed.

loop_families.description: Wake on cadence or material drift, then choose the highest-leverage eligible loop family and workstream.
loop_families.title: Governor and Scheduling

loop_families.description: Refresh truth snapshots, contract-healer artifacts, reconciliation reports, and provider/runtime evidence before planning or publication.
loop_families.title: Evidence Refresh

loop_families.description: Reclassify all open discrepancies into explicit workstreams, dispositions, and blocker states.
loop_families.title: Classification and Backlog

loop_families.description: Advance implementation-authority repairs, registry normalization, packets, ledgers, and prune/freeze follow-through without touching runtime-owned host state.
loop_families.title: Repo-safe Repair Planning

loop_families.description: Prepare, update, and track approval-gated runtime packets without performing freeform host mutation.
loop_families.title: Governed Runtime Packets

loop_families.description: Group bounded checkpoints, validate them, and promote completed work into durable published truth and frozen archive evidence.
loop_families.title: Publication and Freeze

loop_families.description: Keep daily, weekly, monthly, and quarterly maintenance lanes current once active remediation debt is reduced.
loop_families.title: Steady-state Maintenance

success_criteria: C:\Athanor remains the only implementation authority.
No current-root workflow depends on a side root as hidden authority.
Runtime authority remains explicitly governed instead of implicit.
project_exit_gates.title: Authority Gate

success_criteria: Top-entry operator docs agree on the active workstream and posture.
Ralph loop, status, backlog, and operating-system truth do not contradict each other.
Stale outage-era or bootstrap-era prose is removed from active-root surfaces.
project_exit_gates.title: Current-State Truth Gate

success_criteria: Every major runtime drift surface is reconciled, packet-ready, or explicitly externally blocked.
No live host depends on undocumented manual edits.
Packet execution always ends with live re-probe.
project_exit_gates.title: Runtime Gate

success_criteria: Every provider lane is proven, intentionally demoted, blocked for a named reason, or governed handoff only.
Provider catalog, provider evidence, and VAULT env audit agree materially.
No provider remains in a vague probably-works posture.
project_exit_gates.title: Provider Gate

success_criteria: Every in-scope local root has a final disposition.
Every Dirty13itch GitHub repo has a final ecosystem role.
No repo, root, or family remains in maybe-important limbo.
project_exit_gates.title: Portfolio Gate

success_criteria: Field Inspect no longer has an unresolved half-landed replay lane.
RFI and HERS no longer has unresolved stabilization ambiguity.
Product-root reconciliation no longer depends on human memory.
project_exit_gates.title: Product Gate

success_criteria: The validator and generated-doc freshness suite pass from repo authority.
Checkpoint publication stays bounded and repeatable.
Generated reports are trusted outputs instead of stale decoration.
project_exit_gates.title: Validation Gate

success_criteria: Reconciliation work no longer dominates the operating posture.
Ralph-loop execution can shift from active remediation to steady-state monitoring.
The backlog is dominated by maintenance and planned evolution instead of ambiguity cleanup.
project_exit_gates.title: Steady-State Gate

conditions: No non-steady-state exit gate remains in active remediation.
No stale-doc contradiction remains across the top-entry truth surfaces.
Validation and generated-doc checks pass cleanly.
No new authority, provider, or portfolio ambiguity reopens on consecutive Ralph cycles.

success_levels.description: The reconciliation project can be declared closed without hidden authority or ambiguity debt.
success_levels.title: Hard Closure

success_levels.description: Athanor runs as a low-drift, auditable, and governable platform in daily use.
success_levels.title: Operational Success

success_levels.description: The system moves from remediation mode into ongoing Ralph-loop maintenance without reopening ambiguity.
success_levels.title: Steady-state Transition

standing_rules: Do not publish from a stale local mainline.
Do not merge side roots wholesale.
Do not promote runtime-owned host state into implementation authority without corroborating registry or live evidence.
Do not leave any repo, local root, or runtime lane in a 'maybe important' state once reviewed.
Do not preserve stale startup guidance once replacement truth is verified.

exit_criteria: C:\Athanor is the only active implementation-authority root.
origin/main and local mainline are explicitly aligned or drift is packeted.
Startup docs do not route operators or agents to shadow roots.
workstreams.objective: Keep Athanor implementation truth singular, align local work to GitHub mainline, and prevent side roots or stale local history from becoming shadow authority.
workstreams.title: Authority and Mainline Convergence

exit_criteria: High-confidence repo-side deployment drift is closed.
Remaining live mismatches are explicitly classified as runtime-owned host state or approval-gated runtime repair.
workstreams.objective: Bring repo-side Ansible and deployment contracts into line with live evidence where the evidence is strong, and push remaining mismatches into explicit runtime-owned drift packets.
workstreams.title: Deployment Authority Reconciliation

exit_criteria: Runtime-owned surfaces are packeted or mirror-clean.
No host-level mutation path depends on undocumented operator memory.
workstreams.objective: Execute only approval-gated runtime repairs and sync operations through packeted, backup-first paths on DEV, FOUNDRY, WORKSHOP, and VAULT.
workstreams.title: Runtime Sync and Governed Packets

exit_criteria: Auth-failed provider lanes are either repaired or deliberately demoted.
Provider evidence, billing posture, and env contracts no longer disagree.
workstreams.objective: Repair or explicitly demote auth-failed provider lanes and keep secret-surface truth, provider truth, and LiteLLM runtime evidence coherent.
workstreams.title: Provider and Secret Remediation

exit_criteria: Repo-side monitoring truth follows platform-topology and operator-surface truth.
Remaining live Prometheus drift is classified as runtime-owned config shape, not repo ambiguity.
workstreams.objective: Make Prometheus, Grafana alerts, and operator-surface monitoring follow registry-backed service truth instead of stale shadow targets or historical topology assumptions.
workstreams.title: Monitoring and Observability Truth

exit_criteria: Every in-scope local root has a governed disposition.
Every Dirty13itch repo has an explicit ecosystem role.
workstreams.objective: Inventory, classify, and govern every meaningful local root and Dirty13itch GitHub repo under one Athanor ecosystem model.
workstreams.title: Portfolio and Source Reconciliation

exit_criteria: Shared value is imported, queued, archived, or rejected explicitly.
Lineage repos remain non-authoritative after harvest.
workstreams.objective: Mine Local-System, Agentic Coding Tools, AI-Dev-Control-Plane, Reconcile, and athanor-next for reusable value without reviving parallel authorities.
workstreams.title: Lineage and Shared Extraction

exit_criteria: Tenant candidates are classified as light-tenant, deep-tenant, standalone-external, or incubation only.
No meaningful product root remains a floating unmanaged side project.
workstreams.objective: Turn separate product repos and local app roots into an intentional tenant surface with explicit integration depth and platform relationship.
workstreams.title: Tenant Architecture and Classification

exit_criteria: Startup docs point at current truth only.
Superseded active-root docs are archived or deleted after replacement verification.
workstreams.objective: Keep startup docs, runbooks, reference indexes, and archive discipline aligned with current truth while deleting superseded guidance aggressively.
workstreams.title: Startup Docs and Prune

exit_criteria: Validator and generated-doc freshness stay green after each landing.
Reconciliation work is staged into explicit checkpoints with publication readiness.
workstreams.objective: Keep the registry/doc/validator layer green and publish reconciliation work in checkpointed slices instead of another unpublished pileup.
workstreams.title: Validation and Publication
